ab: Ngaay kako a palicay tisowanan haw?
en: A:Can I interview you?

ab: Ngaay to. makahi kako.
en: B: Yes, I would love to.

ab: Ngaay kako a palicay tisowanan haw?
en: A:Can I interview you?

ab: Aka to, cowa ka adihay ko kafana’an ako.
en: B:Don’t, I don’t know much about it.

ab: Ira ko romi’ad iso to harek no lahok haw? sapilisoaw kako tisowanan, ngaay haw?
ch: A:你下午有空嗎?我想去拜訪你可以嗎?
en: A: Are you free this afternoon? I’d like to visit you, is that okay?

ab: Ngaay to, i loma’ sa kako a mitala kisonan.
en: B: Okay, I’ll wait for you at home.

ab: Ira ko romi’ad iso anocila haw? iraay ko lalicayen ako tisowanan to tawcikel no to’as.
ch: A:你明天有空嗎?我想請問你關於阿美族的傳說故事。
en: A: Are you free tomorrow? I’d like to ask you about the legendary story of the [Amis]. *Please change [Amis] to your respective tribe.

ab: tala lotok anocila,. awa kako i loma’.
ch: B:明天我要去山上，不在家喔。
en: B: I’m going to the mountains tomorrow, so I won’t be home.

ab: O matengilay i, mafanaay kiso to kakawa no litengan ,o sapilicay no mako tisowanan.
ch: A:聽說您對阿美族的傳統習俗非常了解，想要請教您一下。
en: A: I heard that you are very familiar with the traditional customs of the Amis, so I would like to ask you a few questions.

ab: co kaadihay ko kafana’an no mako, kawrira i pacaofen ako kiso to kafanaan ako.
ch: B:我所了解的不多，但是我會盡量回答。
en: B: I don’t know much about it, but I will try to answer as best I can.

ab: ngaay kako milukuing haw? nga ngaay a lisopsopen to ikol.
ch: A:請問我可以錄影嗎?這樣以後比較容易記錄。
en: A: Can I record the video? It will be easier to record in the future.

ab: Hayi, ngaay to.
en: B: Okay, no problem.

ab: Pina to ko mihecaan no miso?
en: A: How old are you?

ab: Siwa polo’ ira ko falo to ko mihecaan no mako.
en: B: I’m 98 years old.

ab: Kaemang kiso aki.
en: A: You’re so young.

ab: cowa da, malitengay to kako.
en: B: No, I’m not! I’m old.

ab: Nani cowaay kiso?
en: A: Where are you from?

ab: Nani Posongay kako.
en: B: I’m from [Matthew’s Saddle]. *Please change [Matthew Saddle] to an ethnic location.

ab: O ‘Amis ko fafahi no miso haw?
ch: A:請問您的太太也是阿美族人嗎?
en: A: Is your wife also [Amis]? *Please change \"Amis\" to your ethnic group.

ab: Cowa, o Ponong cingra.
en: B: No, she is a Bunun.

ab: Mafana’ kiso somowal to rama a caciyaw no finacadan haw?
en: A: What languages do you speak?

ab: Mafana’ kako caciyaw to sowal no ‘Amis ato no Ponong.
ch: B: 我會說阿美語和布農語。
en: B: I can speak Ami and Bunun.

ab: Tada mahemek ko faloco’ a palicay tisowanan, pakayni tona miadopay a lisin ngaay haw a sowalen?
ch: A:今天很高興能訪問您，請您談一下，有關狩獵的禁忌好嗎?
en: A: It’s a pleasure to interview you today, can you talk about the taboo of hunting?

ab: Hayi, kako a miadop i kaemang ho.
ch: B:好的，我年輕的時候很常上山打獵。
en: B: Yes. I used to go hunting in the mountains when I was young.

ab: Akowiday, fangcal ko pakafana’ no miso.
ch: A:謝謝，您今天所教導的真是太棒了。
en: A: Thank you, what you taught me today was really great.

ab: soelinay ko sowal no mako to kimad no to’as.
ch: B:我所説的都是千真萬確的，祖先所流傳的講述。
en: B: All that I say is true, the sayings of my ancestors.

ab: tahini aca ko palicay i tisowanan, Akowiday han ako kiso to paini no miso.
ch: A:今天的訪談就到這邊。非常謝謝您的分享。
en: A: That’s all for today’s interview. Thank you very much for sharing with us.

ab: Aka ngodo, romiad sato a miholol.
en: B: You’re welcome, let’s talk again.

ab: Miliyas to kako, ikol to i, nanay matengil ko adihay a kimad no miso.
ch: A:我要離開了，期待下次聽到您更多的故事。
en: A: I’m going to leave now and look forward to hearing more stories next time.

ab: Hai, naonen ko rakat.
en: B: Okay! Please take care.
